title: IBM says AI may replace work tied to 7,800 back-office roles
summary: Reuters reported IBM would pause hiring for back-office functions that the company believed AI and automation could absorb over time, making labor substitution pressure explicit rather than implied.
reviewNote: Accepted as a high-signal enterprise labor event based on corroborated reporting; kept as a shock rather than a slow variable because it is discrete and board-visible.

title: First EU AI Act obligations begin applying
summary: The first operative obligations under the EU AI Act start to apply, adding compliance and governance friction to some workplace AI deployments across the European market.
reviewNote: Accepted as a durable regulatory friction event because the date is fixed in the official legal text and materially affects deployment constraints.
